zh.lifeSummary: Joanna Davis於1624年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼。她於1645年10月26日與David Thomas Jr在美國麻薩諸塞州艾可薩萊姆結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1688年4月17日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可哈維爾爾享年64歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Joanna Davis于1624年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼。她于1645年10月26日与David Thomas Jr在美国马萨诸塞州艾可薩萊姆结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1688年4月17日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可哈維爾爾享年64岁。

nameMeanings.name: Davis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: patronymic meaning ‘Dafydd's (son)’, equivalent to Welsh ap Dafydd, the Welsh form of David . The spelling Davis is more typical in southwestern England northwards as far as Lancashire, where the frequency of the surname largely reflects Welsh migration, but may sometimes represent a native English surname based on Davy (compare Davies ). Davis (including in the sense 2 below) is the eighth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans.
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Gaelic Mac Daibhéid ‘son of David’; see McDevitt . Compare Davies .

History: John Davis or Davys (c. 1550–1605) was an English navigator who searched for the Northwest Passage. — By the 18th century there were numerous persons named Davis in America, including the jurist John Davis, born in 1761 in Plymouth, MA, and Henry Davis, a clergyman and college president, who was born in 1771 in East Hampton, NY. — Jefferson Davis, born in 1808 in KY, was president of the Confederate States of America from 1861 to 1865.

variants.name: David
meanings: Jewish, Welsh, Scottish, English, French, Breton, Portuguese, Czech, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Dávid), Slovak (mainly Dávid): from the Hebrew personal name David (in Hungarian and Slovak spelled Dávid), interpreted as ‘beloved’. The name has been perennially popular among Jews, in honor of the Biblical king of this name. His prominence, and the vivid narrative of his life contained in the First Book of Samuel, led to adoption of the name among Christians in the Middle Ages in various parts of Europe. In Britain, the popularity of this as a personal name was increased for two reasons. Firstly by virtue of its being the name of the patron saint of Wales who was abbot-bishop in the 6th century at what became known as Saint David's in Pembrokeshire. There are numerous dedications and placenames honouring the saint in south Wales, and it is no coincidence that the modern surname is heavily concentrated there, especially in Glamorgan. Secondly, the name was borne by two kings of Scotland (David I, reigning 1124–53, and David II, 1329–71). Its popularity in Russia is largely due to the fact that this was the ecclesiastical name adopted by Saint Gleb (died 1015), one of two sons of Prince Vladimir of Kiev who were martyred for their Christian zeal. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from some other languages, especially Assyrian/Chaldean Dawid and Arabic Daud (with variants, such as Daoud and Dawood ).

History: Guillaume David from France married Marie Armand in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1656. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Davies
meanings: Welsh and English: variant of Davis , a patronymic equivalent to Welsh ap Dafydd ‘Dafydd's son’. The name is especially numerous in south Wales. This form of the name probably shows the influence of Davy , the common English form of the personal name David in the late medieval and early modern periods (see Davey ).

variants.name: Daves
meanings: Welsh and English: probably a variant of   Davis   or   Davies  .
In some cases also German (Däves): from a shortened form of the personal name Matthäus (see Matthew ). This surname is very rare in Germany.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of German   Tewes  , a cognate of 2 above.

nameMeanings.name: Joanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

variants.name: Joannah
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

variants.name: Joana
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

en.lifeSummary: Joanna Davis was born in 1624, in Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. She married David Thomas Jr on 26 October 1645, in Salem, Essex, Massachusetts, United States. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 17 April 1688, in Haverhill, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 64.

fr.lifeSummary: Joanna Davis est née en 1624, à Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Elle a épousé David Thomas Jr le 26 octobre 1645, à Salem, Essex, Massachusetts, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 17 avril 1688, à Haverhill, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 64 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Joanna Davis wurde 1624 in Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America geboren. Sie hat am 26. Oktober 1645 in Salem, Essex, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten David Thomas Jr geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 17. April 1688 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Haverhill, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

it.lifeSummary: Joanna Davis è nata il nel 1624 nel Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Lei sposato David Thomas Jr il 26 ottobre 1645, nel Salem, Essex, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 17 aprile 1688, nel Haverhill, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 64 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joanna Davisは1624年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーで生まれた。1645年10月26日にアメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州エセックスサレムでDavid Thomas Jrと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1688年4月17日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーエセックスヘイバーヒルで64歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joanna Davis(은)는 1624년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1645년 10월 26일 미국 매사추세츠 주 에세스 살렘에서 David Thomas Jr(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1688년 4월 17일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 하버힐에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Joanna Davis nasceu em 1624, em Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Ela casou-se com David Thomas Jr em 26 de outubro de 1645, em Salem, Essex, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 17 de abril de 1688, em Haverhill, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 64 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Joanna Davis родилась в 1624 г. в местности массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени David Thomas Jr 26 октября 1645 г. в местности сеилем, эссекс, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 17 апреля 1688 г. в местности хавирилл, эссекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 64 лет.
